body.title: [triage] A proxy that CAN be bypassed is not a chokepoint — 40 files open raw psycopg2 connections, 6 open raw HTTP to Hex. The bypasses were never unnoticed; they were ticketed at 0.4 and deferred forever.
body.intention: I intend that there is exactly ONE pipe to each shared resource, and that going around it is IMPOSSIBLE rather than discouraged — so that metering, admission control, and the causal record of every call are properties of the substrate and not of everyone remembering the rule. [intention_date: 2026-07-14]
body.description:
**AKIEN, 2026-07-14:** *"we built a database proxy. EVERYONE IS SUPPOSED TO USE THE DATABASE PROXY FOR EVERYTHING. JUST LIKE THE INFERENCE PROXY IS SUPPOSED TO BE THE ONLY PIPE FOR INFERENCE. Your error means that you've been putting in the code, not just in this case, but in most cases, direct db calls. yes? FAIL. CC--."*

He is right. MEASURED 2026-07-14, and I am not rounding it down:

    unseen_university/db_proxy.py  (make_home_proxy / make_local_proxy / make_infra_proxy / make_dc_proxy)
    it logs sql + elapsed_ms + owner, keeps a 500-deep ring, writes slow_queries.log, raises alarms.

    USED BY   : 20 shipped files, nearly all in the LEGACY devices/igor/ tree.
    BYPASSED BY: 40 live-rack files calling psycopg2.connect() directly.

The split is not random. **The newer the code, the more likely it bypasses.** The bypassers are
everything built since the igor era: bus/pg_bus.py, inference/budget_ledger.py, inference/budget_gate.py,
inference/capability_graph.py, inference/pattern_intercept.py, intent/store.py, vault/store.py,
web_server/server.py, channel.py, action_log.py, classifier/{device,annotator}.py, evaluator/device.py,
auditor/device.py, archivist/contrib.py, build_digester/digest_store.py, hubert/constraint_normalizer.py,
installer/global_kb.py, nanny/sweeps/code_sweep.py, scraps/*, librarian/* (13 files).
(devices/postgres/{device,shim}.py connect raw LEGITIMATELY — they ARE the postgres device; its health
probe cannot go through a proxy that needs the DB to be up. That exception is the only one.)

**INFERENCE HAS TWO BYPASS LEVELS, AND ONLY THE SHALLOWER ONE WAS TICKETED:**

    L1  raw HTTP, never enters InferenceDevice AT ALL  -- 6 files -- **UNTICKETED UNTIL NOW**
        aider/shim.py, summarizer/device.py, classifier/annotator.py,
        librarian/research.py, librarian/tools/budget_tools.py, vetinari/device.py
    L2  enters the proxy, skips the DOMAIN layer       -- 10 files -- ticketed:
        T-route-all-inference-through-a-domain (0.8, dependency)

**THE REAL DEFECT IS NOT THE BYPASSES. IT IS HOW THEY WERE PRICED.** They were never unnoticed. They
were noticed, ticketed one file at a time, and each priced too cheap to ever run:

    0.40  Librarian: route its inference through the Proxy + domain router (3 bypasses)
    0.35  Route distillation/factual_compression/consolidation raw ollama.chat through the Proxy
    0.40  Route consult.py _call_openrouter through the Inference Proxy
    0.50  Fix: Use memory_get MCP instead of psycopg2.connect()            [acked]

Each instance is individually too small to prioritize, so ALL of them wait forever. **The PRINCIPLE
— 'the proxy is the only pipe' — has no ticket, therefore no priority, therefore no enforcement.**
Death by a thousand 0.4s. Filing another per-file ticket would repeat the mistake exactly.

**WHAT THE BYPASS COST, MEASURED TODAY — this is not hypothetical:**
  - Akien: *"every inference call is supposed to be tracked, logged both up and downstream, so you
    should be able to go and inspect those."* **I could not.** The io corpus and all 804 RunRecords
    are blind to the 356 Hex connections the test suite made — because they never entered the pipe.
  - I could not count, attribute, or rate-limit Hex's load. Nine of my own orphaned pytest runs
    saturated its single -np 1 slot for hours and produced TWO confidently-wrong diagnoses and two
    false artifacts in the memory store before the load was traced back to us.
  - I fell back to sampling `ss` once per second for connections that live 2-4ms, got a clean zero,
    and reported it as proof. An instrument too coarse to see the event is not evidence of absence.
  - One suite run opened **10,244 Postgres connections** — a connection per operation, no pool,
    nobody counting. I only know the number because my netns router counted them AT THE KERNEL.

**I built a bubblewrap netns with nftables counters to recover, at the kernel, the observability a
chokepoint would have given for free.** That is the measure of the debt.

**THIS TICKET IS THE ENFORCEMENT, NOT THE MIGRATION.** Rewriting 40 files is drudge and it rots
straight back — nothing stops the 41st. The lever is that the TESTER NOW OWNS THE NETWORK
(T-tester-owns-the-network, shipped): inside its netns we can DENY 5432 and 11434 outright and permit
them ONLY via the proxy's path. A bypasser then cannot open the socket. Not 'fails lint' — **cannot
run.** That converts 'everyone is supposed to use the proxy' from a rule people remember into a
property of the kernel, which is the same move that fixed the Hex saturation.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/db_proxy.py; the 40 direct-connect callers above; the 6 raw-HTTP
callers above; unseen_university/devices/tester/netpolicy.py (the enforcement); a new import/callgraph guard.

**Scope boundary:** do NOT change what any caller ASKS FOR (its SQL, its model, its tier). Only change
HOW IT GETS THERE. A migration that alters a query or a prompt has exceeded this ticket. And do not
rewrite a caller's DB URL to change its transport — that silently changes the AUTH METHOD (host/password
-> local/peer), which is precisely the bug that cost 400 test failures today (commit 88f839aa).

**Design step (this is L — break it down before building):** decide (a) whether enforcement is netns-DENY,
an import guard, a monkeypatch tripwire in conftest, or all three; (b) whether the DB proxy pools (10,244
connections says it must); (c) what the ONE legitimate exception list is (postgres device health probe) and
how it is declared rather than assumed.

**Completion criteria:** (1) a guard proves NO module outside the proxy layer calls psycopg2.connect or
opens HTTP to an inference endpoint, with the postgres-device exception DECLARED not special-cased;
(2) a test running in the tester's netns with 5432/11434 DENIED still passes, because everything reaches
them through the proxy's permitted path — a bypasser physically cannot; (3) the io corpus records the
inference calls the suite makes, so Akien's 'you should be able to go and inspect those' is TRUE.

**Test plan:** red->green. RED: the guard test lists today's 40 + 6 bypassers and fails. GREEN: the list
is empty and the netns run passes with both ports denied. The hollow build this must fail: a lint rule
nobody runs, or a guard with a growing allowlist — an allowlist IS the escape hatch (CP1).

**Absorbs / supersedes:** the four scattered per-file tickets above should be re-parented to this one
or closed into it — they are instances, this is the class.

**Relates to:** T-route-all-inference-through-a-domain (L2 — this ticket is the layer BELOW it),
T-constrained-resources-need-an-owner-with-admission-control (UNBUILDABLE without this: you cannot
admission-control a resource that six files dial directly), T-io-corpus-erases-the-cause-of-every-failure
(the corpus cannot record calls that never enter the pipe), T-tester-owns-the-network (the enforcement lever).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
